input.command:
python3 -c "
import json
# Search Feb 26 articles for key evidence
with open('articles/2026/02/26/articles.jsonl') as f:
    lines = f.readlines()
print(f'Feb 26 articles: {len(lines)}')
for line in lines:
    a = json.loads(line)
    title = (a.get('title','') or '').lower()
    content = (a.get('content','') or '').lower()[:2000]
    checks = [
        ('iran geneva', 'iran' in title and ('geneva' in content[:500] or 'talk' in content[:300] or 'collaps' in content[:500] or 'fail' in content[:500] or 'break' in content[:500])),
        ('t20 results', 't20' in title and ('south africa' in content[:500] or 'west indies' in content[:500] or 'india' in content[:500])),
        ('world cup iran', ('world cup' in title and 'iran' in content[:500]) or ('iran' in title and 'world cup' in content[:500])),
    ]
    for label, match in checks:
        if match:
            print(f'[{label}] {(a.get(\"title\",\"\") or \"\")[:120]}')
            print(f'  {(a.get(\"content\",\"\") or \"\")[:300]}')
            print()
            break
" 2>/dev/null | head -80

blocks.content:
New Zealand fight back to eliminate Sri Lanka from T20 World Cup

===
New Zealand fight back to eliminate co-hosts Sri Lanka from cricket T20 World Cup
COLOMBO – New Zealand recovered from a poor start to crush Sri Lanka by 61 runs in cricket’s Twenty20 World Cup Super Eights and eliminate the co-hosts from the tournament on Feb 25.
Mitchell Santner led New Zealand’s recovery with a captain’s knock of 47 off 26 balls after coming in with his side in huge trouble at 84-5, to power them to a competitive 168-7 at the R. Premadasa Stadium.
Sri Lanka
